statement: Use source-first enrichment: confirm a trusted source and exact part-number match before extracting facts; unresolved cases should fail closed to review instead of generating fake specificity.
notes: Stable core rule derived from PEHA recovery and broader naming cleanup.

statement: Preferred source order is official manufacturer page or PDF, then large B2B distributor with exact PN, then technical B2B catalog with exact match; repo artifacts are hints only, while marketplace, SEO, and auction pages are weak hints only.
notes: Use this as default trust hierarchy for future enrichment research.

statement: Existing repo artifacts such as old generated titles, xlsx or csv outputs, product_data.json, and cached pages are useful hints, but they must not be treated as canonical truth without stronger confirmation.
notes: Important guard against self-reinforcing naming errors.

statement: Onboard a new brand by finding 1-2 anchor SKU with strong repo or source clues, deriving family and type coding patterns from those anchors, expanding the pattern to the cluster, then using external lookup only for unknown clusters and individual lookup only for edge cases.
notes: This is the main reusable research method that made PEHA tractable.

statement: Generic titles must not survive when family-specific product types are known; for example, placeholders such as 'Электроустановочный модуль ...' should route to review or be replaced by family-aware titles.
notes: Generalized from PEHA, but the rule applies to any brand with coded families.

statement: PEHA requires a family-aware naming approach: generic naming is unsafe; 20.67 maps to NOVA, 20.57 maps to AURA, 95.57 maps to DIALOG, 95.6511 maps to DIALOG socket, and TV/UAE/USB/TAE/RADIO/MP3 tokens usually indicate insert.
notes: Curated stable summary from the PEHA batch and external verification.

statement: Within PEHA NOVA, codes 20.671, 20.672, 20.673, 20.674, and 20.675 map deterministically to frame gang counts 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5.
notes: Useful as a direct deterministic extraction rule when exact family match is confirmed.

statement: Separate discovery from processing by storing scout findings in a cache or append-only artifact; manual Codex scout and future API scout should write compatible structured findings that the pipeline can read later.
notes: Derived from naming_scout_cache work; helps bridge manual scout now and API scout later.

statement: Introduce new naming logic in shadow mode first, compare new and old titles, then enable by family allowlist while keeping legacy title assembly as fallback.
notes: Safer rollout pattern for future brand onboarding.

statement: A Russian storefront title must not silently ship with untranslated English product-type or attribute fragments; mixed-language output is a naming-quality defect and should route to correction or review rather than silent acceptance.
notes: Series names, model codes, and canonical branded code-names may stay as-is; the defect is loss of Russian product wording or stray English fragments in the final RU title.

statement: If the final title loses the confirmed product type, family, series, or another key confirmed identifier needed to distinguish the item, the result is an under-specified naming defect and should not be treated as a normal final title.
notes: Especially important for coded families where the difference between frame, rocker, socket, insert, or decorative element materially changes the product meaning.

statement: When a trusted source confirms an important series, family, or coded product name, the final title must preserve that signal; dropping it is a naming defect, especially if the remaining title becomes generic.
notes: Triggered repeatedly on PEHA-family work where loss of NOVA/AURA/DIALOG or equivalent coded family meaning degraded title quality.

statement: Generic fallback titles are not a normal success state when family-aware naming should have materialized; they are acceptable only as explicit low-confidence fallback under strict review-oriented conditions.
notes: This complements the anti-generic rule: if family-aware resolution was expected but generic fallback survived, treat it as a controlled fallback state, not as a good final title.

statement: Exact attributes such as color, material, orientation, or narrow subtype must not be inserted into the final title unless they are confirmed strongly enough; weak or conflicting hints must stay out of the title.
notes: Triggered by repeated cases where external sources disagreed on finish or where subtype inference was weaker than family and product-type confirmation.

statement: A final storefront title must be self-contained: it should identify the product by exact RU product type plus brand and any confirmed family, series, subbrand, or model signal, rather than relying on part number alone.
notes: Use this as the default naming-quality rule for product-card titles across mixed Honeywell slices and similar brands.

statement: A final title may include differentiating attributes only when they are confirmed strongly enough and materially help distinguish the SKU; unconfirmed or decorative attributes must stay out of the title.
notes: Examples include interface, size, packaging, belt, corded, wireless, DN, Kvs, dpi, and similar disambiguating traits.

statement: Apply title corrections in batch by resolving exact product type and model/family signals first, assembling titles from confirmed differentiators only, then validating for RU type presence, brand presence, preserved model signal, PN suffix, and non-generic output before acceptance.
notes: This is the operational checklist behind Naming Standard v1 and should be used for future card-title cleanup batches.

statement: Treat temporary placeholder photos as a separate merchandising layer, not as identity proof: approximate, family-level, or otherwise non-exact images may be acceptable for initial selling if they are tracked as temporary placeholders and scheduled for later replacement with real product photography.
notes: This preserves strict evidence rules while allowing faster storefront population with temporary imagery.

statement: Local AI may be used to improve temporary placeholder photos for clarity, cleanup, background, or upscale quality, but such enhancement must not be treated as exact product evidence and must not invent SKU-specific details that were not present in the source image.
notes: Use enhancement to improve presentation only; exact identity still depends on trusted product evidence and later real photography remains the replacement target.

statement: Placeholder photos must remain a separate merchandising layer and must never be counted as SKU identity proof, even when they are acceptable as temporary storefront imagery.
notes: This hardens the separation between image evidence and temporary merchandising assets.

statement: Use a narrow photo-status contract with exact_evidence, family_evidence, placeholder, and rejected; placeholder may support temporary storefront presentation, but it must not upgrade image evidence status or satisfy auto-publish image requirements.
notes: Derived from placeholder-photo rollout planning; designed to avoid a second truth layer while enabling temporary catalog coverage.

statement: When API search is unavailable or fragile, Codex may manually scout exact product pages on trusted or reviewable domains and hand off the page URLs to a deterministic asset runner that parses the page, resolves the asset URL, downloads the file, and records trust plus structured-PN evidence separately.
notes: This keeps search flexible without introducing browser automation as a default dependency; downloaded assets still require normal image-quality and identity review.

statement: When live search is unavailable or intentionally out of scope, a bounded price-only scout pilot may seed candidate pages from prior admissible price evidence and source-surface evidence, then re-run deterministic price extraction on that seeded slice without SerpAPI.
notes: This keeps the pilot bounded and lets the team test the extraction seam separately from broader search-provider expansion.

statement: A quota failure on the LLM extraction step must be recorded separately from price-source lineage quality: a batch may still prove exact product lineage and zero source-surface conflicts even when live price extraction collapses to no_price_found because the provider quota is exhausted.
notes: Captured from the bounded N3 pilot where 15 seeded Honeywell SKU preserved exact product lineage with zero surface conflicts but 429 insufficient_quota prevented live price extraction.

statement: When provider quota blocks LLM price extraction, Codex may seed a bounded price batch manually with exact product-page URLs plus observed public-price fields, and the pipeline should still re-fetch the page and enforce deterministic exact-lineage and source-surface checks before accepting the result.
notes: Captured from the manual price proof batch that closed 5 Honeywell SKU with public_price=5/5 and zero surface conflicts without OpenAI API dependency.

statement: Exact product pages on different locale or mirror hosts of the same distributor must not be silently treated as the same price surface; if source-surface stability already points to one admissible host, a new locale host should be reviewed as a replacement surface rather than auto-merged.
notes: Captured from Honeywell 1006186: at.rs-online.com and twen.rs-online.com were both exact RS pages, but the batch had to preserve the previously stabilized twen surface to avoid a real surface-conflict event.

statement: A bounded manual price path should be judged on a mixed robustness slice, not only on perfect public-price rows: the healthy pattern is exact lineage on the full slice, zero source-surface conflicts, zero transient failures, and an honest distribution across public_price, ambiguous_offer, and no_price_found outcomes.
notes: Captured from the 20-SKU Honeywell robustness batch with exact_product_lineage_confirmed_count=20, surface_conflict_count=0, transient_failure_row_count=0, and mixed statuses rather than a cherry-picked all-green slice.

statement: When an exact product page on a weak or unknown tier exposes a visible public price, the price may still be harvested as observed data, but policy must downgrade the outcome to ambiguous_offer instead of treating it as a normal admissible public_price.
notes: Captured from the robustness slice on 00020211, 1006187, 1012539, and 1015021: exact lineage stayed true, but weak/unknown tier public prices were intentionally review-routed rather than silently accepted.

statement: A local photo enhancement layer may generate cleaner catalog-friendly placeholder derivatives from canonical raw product photos only if the output stays explicitly placeholder, remains non-generative, preserves raw-to-derivative lineage, and never upgrades image evidence truth.
notes: Captured from the first bounded photo_enhance proof batch: deterministic PIL transforms produced derivative placeholder assets under downloads/photos_enhanced while the raw canonical photo and evidence contract stayed unchanged.

statement: After a bounded proof batch validates the raw-to-derivative contract, widen photo enhancement by reusing the same safe non-generative profile across more canonical raw photos before introducing stronger AI variation or a second enhancement path.
notes: Captured from the target20 expansion decision: practical coverage should be increased with the stable v1 cleanup/presentation profile first, and only later should the team consider more aggressive AI-based variation if the safe profile proves insufficient.

statement: If a raw product photo is already marked REJECT in the photo verdict cache, photo enhancement must not materialize or preserve it as a valid placeholder output; instead the run should emit rejected status and flag any old derivative as a cleanup candidate.
notes: Captured from the all25 recheck: known bad images such as logos, banners, thumbnails, and user-confirmed mismatches are now blocked before photo_enhance output and old generated copies are surfaced as stale rather than silently accepted.

statement: Descriptions, site placement, and product-type values from the local InSales import may be overlaid onto catalog bundles as seeded merchandising content, but that refresh must stay additive: it should not silently rebuild upstream price or identity evidence when the bounded task is only content/export improvement.
notes: Captured from the 25-SKU Honeywell refresh: import content was safely promoted into canonical export while evidence decisions stayed anchored to a known-good base slice and only explicit photo-verdict overrides changed status.

statement: If the current photo verdict for a SKU is REJECT, export must not fall back to the raw local photo path; the image field should stay blank unless a separate accepted merchandising placeholder is attached explicitly.
notes: Captured from the canonical export refresh: rejected photo rows such as 010130.10, 027913.10, 1011893-RU, and 1050000000 now export with blank image fields instead of silently reusing bad raw photos.

statement: When currency normalization is unavailable for a source-price currency (for example TWD or other non-covered currencies), the result row must carry fx_normalization_status=fx_gap and fx_gap_reason_code=fx_rate_unavailable_for_currency instead of a silent null in the rub_price field. This makes conversion gaps traceable and distinguishable from genuine zero or missing prices.
notes: Cross-reference: ENGINEERING-EXPLICIT-FX-GAP-STATUS-001 in engineering memory. Surfaced in the N3 20-SKU robustness batch where TWD price stayed acceptable as a source currency but rub_price required an explicit gap marker rather than silent null. Enrichment agents must not treat fx_gap rows as no_price_found; they are price_found_with_conversion_gap.

statement: When top-level bundle card_status is AUTO_PUBLISH but policy_decision_v2 resolves to REVIEW_REQUIRED or DRAFT_ONLY, the SKU must be downgraded via an explicit holdout marker (LEGACY_AUTO_PUBLISH_HOLDOUT) and not published. The holdout must be recorded in both the evidence bundle (review_reasons, refresh_trace.holdout_applied) and the export CSV, and the reason must be human-readable. This applies whenever the bundle card_status pre-dates the _mirror_bundle_card_outcome contract and was not re-derived from v2.
notes: Captured from Pack A cleanup gate: 6 SKU (033588.17, 1000106, 1006186, 1006187, 1012539, 1061200000) had AUTO_PUBLISH in legacy evidence but REVIEW_REQUIRED in v2 policy. Owner decision Q1 confirmed: downgrade to REVIEW_REQUIRED, no publication, holdout marker in evidence. AUTO_PUBLISH=0 became the invariant for the 25-SKU canonical slice.

statement: Evidence bundles produced before _mirror_bundle_card_outcome was introduced may carry a top-level card_status that differs from policy_decision_v2.card_status. This mismatch is known legacy debt, not a pipeline bug. The correct treatment is: (1) surface the mismatch count explicitly in refresh_trace (policy_card_status_mismatch_count), (2) preserve the bundle as-is for operational continuity, (3) schedule a bounded cleanup batch to re-derive card_status from v2 for affected SKU. Do not silently hide or auto-correct the mismatch.
notes: Surfaced in the 25-SKU canonical refresh: policy_card_status_mismatch_count=11 was made explicit in refresh_trace instead of being hidden. Full cleanup deferred to a separate bounded batch after photo/price follow-up queues are worked. Pre-mirror evidence bundles remain valid for all non-publication operations.

statement: A publicly visible price on an official or authorized distributor page is a valid reference price signal even when the product is marked discontinued or out of stock. The pipeline must not downgrade or discard it solely because stock_status=discontinued. The correct treatment is: record stock_status=discontinued, keep price_status=public_price if the price is genuinely visible on the page, and note in price_basis_note that the price is a last-known retail figure not an active offer. This signal is useful as a price floor for procurement and market context even if the SKU is no longer actively sold.
notes: Confirmed from 1011994 Leightning L1N on honeywellstore.com: $9.95 USD, discontinued, tier=official. Script returned public_price, lineage=True, surface_stable=True, review_required=False. The pipeline correctly accepted the price. Rule formalizes that behavior as intentional and reusable.

statement: EU-specific PPE variant SKU (for example Leightning L3H EU adapter variant 1012541 vs US base 1011993, or EU adapter codes 3711/3712/3721) exist on EU regional distributors but are NOT covered by US/UK standard distributors that cover the equivalent US model. Crucially, this is an access-pattern gap, not a source-nonexistence gap: EU product pages exist and can be confirmed via web search snippets, but many EU safety-workwear sites (e.g. safety5000.com, laropadetrabajo.com) are blocked by SSL timeout or bot-protection at the automated-script level. The correct scout strategy is: (1) confirm the US base model PN and price as a reference floor; (2) search for the exact EU PN on regional EU safety/workwear distributors, not on prosafco.be or uvex-safety.com which carry different product families; (3) if script access is blocked, record outcome as snippet_confirmed_script_blocked rather than no_price_found; (4) manual browser verification is the appropriate escalation path, not bot-protection bypass.
notes: Updated from EU slice v1: safety5000.com has exact 1012541 page (ECONNREFUSED); laropadetrabajo.com has exact 1015021 at EUR29.15+IVA (SSL timeout); both confirmed via search snippet. prosafco.be confirmed wrong: carries Honeywell PPE but not Howard Leight Leightning series. uvex-safety.com confirmed wrong domain/brand: sells uvex-brand hearing protection, not Howard Leight. Candidate EU sources: safety5000.com, laropadetrabajo.com. Access requires manual browser or supplier feed, not bot-bypass.
supersedes_note: Earlier version (2026-04-02T16:00) incorrectly listed prosafco.be and uvex-safety.com as priority EU targets. Both proved wrong after EU slice v1.

title: Two distinct RU lineage failure classes: 403-block vs structured_exact_match_missing
observation: Russian sites split into two access-pattern classes. Class A (vseinstrumenti.ru, honeywell.ru): HTTP 403 — script completely blocked. Class B (bionic.spb.ru, gwardex.ru, kovkapro.com): HTTP 200, HTML delivered, but script returns lineage=False with lineage_reason_code=structured_exact_match_missing. These are different problems requiring different fixes.
lesson: When lineage=False and http_status=200, check lineage_reason_code. If 'structured_exact_match_missing' the page is accessible — the issue is script pattern coverage, not bot-protection. This is fixable via script improvement without any bot-bypass.
apply_when: Classifying RU domain access failures. Do not conflate 403-class and structured-match-class. 403 = escalate to owner manual browser. structured_match_missing = candidate for script pattern improvement (LOW risk task).

title: vseinstrumenti.ru is the primary RU public-price source for Leightning helmet-attachment PPE
observation: After searching 12 Russian domains for Honeywell Leightning L1HHV (1015021) and L3H EU (1012541): only vseinstrumenti.ru has confirmed public prices (4314/5356 RUB). bionic.spb.ru has both SKUs but rfq_only. gwardex.ru has related SKUs (L1H 4250.60 RUB in_stock) but not these two. Other sites (technoavia.ru stale 404, kovkapro.com L2 only, sizproject.ru VeriShield only).
lesson: The Leightning helmet-adapter series (L1HHV, L3H EU) is narrowly distributed in Russia — vseinstrumenti.ru dominates public pricing. For other Leightning variants (L1, L1H, L2), gwardex.ru is a script-accessible alternative.
apply_when: Choosing RU sources for Honeywell PPE enrichment. Use vseinstrumenti.ru for 1015021/1012541. Use gwardex.ru for L1H (1012539) and L1 variants. Do not expect wide RU coverage for helmet-specific variants.
